title: cerastium pumilum subsp. algericum
intro: Cerastium pumilum subsp. algericum, also known as Snow-in-summer, is a low-growing, flowering perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which have been utilized to treat various health issues. The plant's health benefits include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and calming nervous system imbalances, making it a popular herbal remedy for digestive issues, skin problems, and anxiety. The herbal preparations of Snow-in-summer include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used externally or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
